$comment: GENERATED — do not edit. Generated by genesis_taxonomy from catalog 'consent_fixture' v0.1.0.
title: updateComponents (consent_fixture catalog v0.1.0)
description: A2UI v0.9-shaped updateComponents message for the consent_fixture catalog. Always emit the WHOLE component tree; the client reconciles by component id. Exactly one component must have id "root".

surfaceId.description: Identifier of the surface being updated.

components.description: Flat adjacency list of components. Containers reference children by id.

oneOf.description: A stateful integer counter leaf. Holds a live count and re-renders it; affords client actions. AFFORDS CLIENT ACTIONS: the client may send an A2UI action message with sourceComponentId set to this component's id and one of these action names: "press" — Increment the count by context.amount (integer, optional, default 1). "set" — Overwrite the count with context.value (integer, required). Last write wins.

id.description: Unique, stable component id. Becomes the reconciliation key: re-emit the same id to update a component in place.

press.description: Increment the count by context.amount (integer, optional, default 1).

set.description: Overwrite the count with context.value (integer, required). Last write wins.

oneOf.description: A named value leaf holding a single string datum. Cannot have children.

id.description: Unique, stable component id. Becomes the reconciliation key: re-emit the same id to update a component in place.

name.description: Name of this datum within the measurement.

value.description: Measured string value at configuration time.

oneOf.description: A named container component. Holds an ordered list of child components referenced by id.

id.description: Unique, stable component id. Becomes the reconciliation key: re-emit the same id to update a component in place.

name.description: Structural name of this node in the measurement.

children.description: Ordered ids of child components. Every id must appear as a component in the same components list.
